# Rocky

> The trust system for your data. A Rust-based control plane for warehouse-side data pipelines: branches, replay, provable reproducibility, column-level lineage, compile-time safety, per-model cost attribution. Keep Databricks or Snowflake. Bring Rocky for the DAG.

Rocky is not a warehouse. Storage and compute stay with your warehouse (Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, DuckDB); Rocky owns the graph ‚Äî dependencies, compile-time types, drift, incremental logic, lineage, cost. No Jinja, no manifest, no parse step.

Scope on the ELT spectrum:
- Extract (SaaS sources) ‚Äî not Rocky; use Fivetran, Airbyte, Stitch, or warehouse-native CDC
- Extract (files) ‚Äî `rocky load` handles CSV / Parquet / JSONL from a directory
- Load (bronze replication) ‚Äî config-driven pipelines via Fivetran metadata / DuckDB information_schema / manual declaration
- Transform ‚Äî compiled SQL models
- Quality ‚Äî inline assertions during `rocky run`
- Orchestration ‚Äî first-class Dagster integration; `rocky serve` for standalone teams

## Architecture at a glance

- **Single binary** (`rocky`), starts in under 100ms.
- **Adapter model** separates *source discovery* (Fivetran, DuckDB, manual) from *warehouse execution* (Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, DuckDB). The core is warehouse-agnostic.
- **Bronze layer** (replication) is driven entirely by `rocky.toml` ‚Äî zero SQL files for 1:1 copies.
- **Silver layer** (transformation) is pure SQL with a sidecar `.toml` for config. No templating language.
- **Rocky DSL** (`.rocky` files) is an optional pipeline-oriented syntax; raw SQL stays first-class.
- **State** (watermarks, run history) lives in a local `redb` database with optional S3 / Valkey sync.
- **JSON output** is versioned and schema-backed ‚Äî designed for orchestrator consumption.

## Monorepo

| Path | Artifact | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `engine/` | `rocky` CLI | 20-crate Rust workspace |
| `integrations/dagster/` | `dagster-rocky` PyPI wheel | Dagster resource + component |
| `editors/vscode/` | Rocky VSIX | LSP client + AI commands |
| `examples/playground/` | Config only | 47 POCs across 8 categories; DuckDB-backed, no credentials |

## CLI commands

### Core pipeline
- `rocky init [path]` ‚Äî Scaffold a new project
- `rocky validate` ‚Äî Check config without network calls
- `rocky discover` ‚Äî List connectors and tables
- `rocky plan [--filter key=value]` ‚Äî Generate SQL without executing
- `rocky run [--filter key=value] [--resume-latest]` ‚Äî Execute the pipeline
- `rocky state` ‚Äî Show stored watermarks
- `rocky load` ‚Äî Bulk load data from files into the warehouse

### Modeling
- `rocky compile` ‚Äî Type-check models, resolve DAG, validate contracts
- `rocky lineage <model>` ‚Äî Column-level lineage
- `rocky test` ‚Äî Run assertions locally via DuckDB
- `rocky ci` ‚Äî Compile + test in one step
- `rocky ci-diff` ‚Äî Diff current vs. base branch for PR feedback
- `rocky dag` / `rocky dag-run` ‚Äî Inspect / execute the DAG
- `rocky drift` ‚Äî Report schema drift between source and target
- `rocky column-lineage <model>` ‚Äî Column-level lineage JSON

### Data quality
- `rocky test` ‚Äî Unit-style assertions (`not_null`, `unique`, `in_range`, `regex_match`, `aggregate`, custom SQL)
- Inline pipeline checks: `row_count`, `column_match`, `freshness`, `null_rate`, `anomaly`, custom (configured in `rocky.toml`)

### AI
- `rocky ai "<intent>"` ‚Äî Generate a model from natural language
- `rocky ai-sync` ‚Äî Detect upstream schema changes, propose updates
- `rocky ai-explain` ‚Äî Generate intent descriptions from SQL
- `rocky ai-test` ‚Äî Generate test assertions from intent

### Administration
- `rocky doctor` ‚Äî Aggregate health check
- `rocky compare` ‚Äî Shadow-vs-production comparison
- `rocky history` ‚Äî Run + model execution history
- `rocky metrics <model>` ‚Äî Quality metrics and trends
- `rocky estimate` ‚Äî Cost / row-count estimation
- `rocky optimize` ‚Äî Materialization strategy recommendations
- `rocky compact <model>` / `rocky compact-dedup` ‚Äî Storage compaction (OPTIMIZE / VACUUM / dedup)
- `rocky archive` ‚Äî Archive old partitions
- `rocky profile-storage` ‚Äî Storage layout analysis
- `rocky seed` ‚Äî Load seed data
- `rocky model-history <model>` ‚Äî History for a single model

### Development
- `rocky playground [path]` ‚Äî Create a credential-free DuckDB project
- `rocky import-dbt` ‚Äî Convert a dbt project to Rocky
- `rocky serve` ‚Äî HTTP API server
- `rocky lsp` ‚Äî Language Server Protocol (IDE integration)
- `rocky hooks list` / `rocky hooks test` ‚Äî Manage lifecycle hooks
- `rocky init-adapter <name>` ‚Äî Scaffold a new warehouse adapter crate
- `rocky test-adapter --adapter <name>` ‚Äî Run conformance tests
- `rocky validate-migration` ‚Äî Validate a migration plan

### Global flags
- `-c, --config <PATH>` ‚Äî config file (default `rocky.toml`)
- `-o, --output <FORMAT>` ‚Äî `json` (default) or `table`
- `--state-path <PATH>` ‚Äî state store path (default `.rocky-state.redb`)

## Materialization strategies

- `full_refresh` ‚Äî `CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE`
- `incremental` ‚Äî `INSERT INTO ... WHERE ts > watermark`
- `merge` ‚Äî `MERGE INTO ... USING ON key`
- `materialized_view` ‚Äî Databricks materialized views
- `dynamic_table` ‚Äî Snowflake dynamic tables with target lag
- `time_interval` ‚Äî Partition-keyed processing with `@start_date` / `@end_date`

## Configuration (`rocky.toml`)

Top-level sections:

- `[adapter.NAME]` ‚Äî named adapter connections (one per warehouse / source)
- `[pipeline.NAME]` ‚Äî named pipelines (one of: `replication`, `transformation`, `quality`, `snapshot`)
- `[pipeline.NAME.source]` / `[pipeline.NAME.target]` ‚Äî pipeline endpoints
- `[pipeline.NAME.source.schema_pattern]` ‚Äî multi-tenant schema name parser
- `[pipeline.NAME.checks]` ‚Äî inline data quality checks
- `[pipeline.NAME.governance]` ‚Äî tags, permissions, workspace isolation
- `[state]` ‚Äî watermark backend (`local`, `s3`, `valkey`)
- `[cache]` / `[cost]` / `[hooks]` ‚Äî optional subsystems

Select pipelines at runtime with `--pipeline NAME`.

## Supported adapters

| Role | Adapter | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Source | Fivetran | Stable |
| Source | DuckDB | Stable |
| Source | Manual (TOML) | Stable |
| Warehouse | Databricks | Stable ‚Äî SQL Statement API, Unity Catalog, adaptive concurrency (AIMD) |
| Warehouse | Snowflake | Beta ‚Äî OAuth, key-pair JWT, password |
| Warehouse | BigQuery | Beta ‚Äî service account, ADC |
| Warehouse | DuckDB | Stable ‚Äî local in-process, used by playground and `rocky test` |

A single DuckDB instance can act as both source and warehouse ‚Äî that is how the playground runs end-to-end without credentials.

## Dagster integration (`dagster-rocky`)

- `RockyResource` ‚Äî Configurable resource wrapping the CLI (20+ typed methods)
- `load_rocky_assets()` ‚Äî Auto-discover Dagster assets from Rocky sources
- `emit_check_results()` / `emit_materializations()` ‚Äî Convert JSON output to Dagster events
- `RockyComponent` ‚Äî State-backed `dg` component for scalable discovery
- `run_streaming()` / `run_pipes()` ‚Äî Streaming + Dagster Pipes
- Autogenerated Pydantic v2 models for every CLI output schema

## AI intent layer

Intent is stored as model metadata in the `.toml` sidecar. Rocky's AI commands operate on that intent:

- Generate a model from a prompt (`rocky ai`)
- Keep models in sync when upstream schemas change (`rocky ai-sync`)
- Backfill intent descriptions from existing SQL (`rocky ai-explain`)
- Derive test assertions from intent (`rocky ai-test`)
